title: Durable sequence bridge capability surface

backends.provider_offset_source: no separate provider offset; portable sequence is the only ordering domain
notes: Replay and checkpoints use the same in-process sequence counter.
No external provider epoch exists for memory-only history.

backends.provider_offset_source: subscriber_group_lease.checkpoint_offset stores the same durable integer domain
notes: Checkpoint monotonicity is enforced against the same durable integer sequence used for replay.
Lease epoch fencing prevents stale writers from regressing the shared checkpoint domain.

backends.portable_sequence_source: remote subscriber checkpoint event_sequence returned by the HTTP event-log service
backends.provider_offset_source: remote durable integer sequence mirrored into checkpoint_offset by the shared lease coordinator
notes: The HTTP lane preserves the same portable sequence semantics while moving the log behind a shared service boundary.
Ownership fencing still relies on the shared lease epoch domain.

backends.portable_sequence_source: local stub sequence counter exposed as Position.Sequence
backends.provider_offset_source: stub-N publish offset resolves back to the same numeric checkpoint_offset domain
backends.mapping_contract: provider offset string resolves to one local integer sequence domain
notes: The stub proves the mapping contract deterministically before a real replicated adapter exists.
Ownership and stale-writer fencing are still evaluated through the shared lease epoch path.

backends.portable_sequence_source: future provider offset or quorum position mapped into Position.Sequence
backends.ownership_epoch_source: future provider-backed ownership epoch plus subscriber lease fencing
backends.mapping_contract: portable monotonic sequence bridge required before rollout-ready claims
notes: No live provider-backed adapter is wired yet.
A real adapter must preserve monotonic replay/checkpoint semantics across failover using one portable sequence contract.

current_ceiling: SQLite and HTTP paths already share one durable integer sequence domain, but broker-backed mapping remains future work.
Subscriber ownership fencing is shared-lease-backed rather than provider-native.
next_runtime_hooks: Expose sequence bridge posture in runtime diagnostics and reviewer payloads.
Thread sequence bridge metadata through subscriber lease status and checkpoint rejection events.
Keep broker_replicated marked contract_only until a real provider-backed adapter proves the mapping against failover.
